title: EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION - 211info
text: INTERMOUNTAIN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Oregon Department of Education contracts with local agencies to provide a statewide system of free services for young children with developmental delays and disabilities and their families, including: -Early Intervention (EI): Individually designed services for children birth to three and support for parents to enhance children's physical, cognitive, communication, social emotional, and/or adaptive development; and -Early Childhood Special Education (ECSE): Specially designed instruction for children ages 3 to the age of public school eligibility in the areas of communication, cognitive, social/emotional, adaptive, and others. 2001 SW Nye Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Any child 0-school age with a suspected developmental delay or disability is eligible for free screening and evaluation. Children determined to have delays or disabilities are eligible for EI/ECSE services. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 966-3218 , , , , ..12.. Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 966-3100 Main phone (888) 437-6892 Toll-free CSE Director (541) 966-3218 Baker, Grant, Morrow, Umatilla, Union and Wallowa Counties Cade Burnette [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Baker, Grant, Malheur, Morrow, Umatilla, Union, and Wallowa Counties Services: Early Identification Programs Early Intervention for Children with Disabilities/Delays Pediatric Developmental Assessment Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - SPECIAL EDUCATION EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - HOME SCHOOLING INFORMATION SERVICES - BLUE MOUNTAIN EARLY LEARNING HUB PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - PRESCHOOL PROMISE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. INTERMOUNTAIN EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT
